info.title: InsightIDR API
info.description:
Introduction

Welcome to the reference documentation for the InsightIDR public APIs.

Here are a few resources to help you learn how to start using our APIs:

  • Learn about basic concepts and capabilities
  • Get an Insight platform API key to set up authentication
  • See available Insight product APIs
  • Learn more about InsightIDR

After you've got the basics down, you can use this API guide to find examples of requests and responses.

tags.name: Accounts
tags.description: An API used to find and search InsightIDR accounts. See https://docs.rapid7.com/insightidr/users-and-accounts-on-your-domain for further information on accounts.

tags.name: Local Accounts
tags.description: An API used to find and search InsightIDR local accounts. See https://docs.rapid7.com/insightidr/users-and-accounts-on-your-domain for further information on local accounts.

tags.name: Assets
tags.description: An API used to find and search InsightIDR assets. See https://docs.rapid7.com/insightidr/assets-on-your-domain for further information on assets.

tags.name: Attachments
tags.description: An API used to upload, list, download and delete attachments. For example, the create API can be used to upload an attachment.

tags.name: Comments
tags.description: An API used to find, create, and delete comments. For example, these APIs can be used to create a comment for a particular investigation.

tags.name: Community Threats
tags.description: An API used to add and replace indicators for Community Threats. See https://insightidr.help.rapid7.com/docs/threats#section-threat-api for further information on how to generate threat keys.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: An API used to find and search InsightIDR users. See https://docs.rapid7.com/insightidr/users-and-accounts-on-your-domain for further information on users.

tags.name: Health Metrics
tags.description: An API used to retrieve health metrics of an organization.

tags.name: Collectors
tags.description: An API used to manage collectors for an organization.

get.description: Retrieves attachments matching the given request parameters. For example, this API allows you to list all attachments by investigation.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Return attachments with this target.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The optional, 0 based index of the page to retrieve.  Must be an integer greater than or equal to 0.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The optional size of the page to retrieve. Must be an integer greater than 0 or less than or equal to 100.

post.description: An API used to upload one or more attachments. By default, attachments are not associated with a comment. Use this API to create comment.

get.description: An API you can use to download an attachment by RRN. The RRN determines which attachment is downloaded.

parameters.name: rrn
parameters.description: The RRN of the attachment.

delete.description: An API you can use to delete an attachment with the given RRN.

parameters.name: rrn
parameters.description: The RRN of the attachment.

get.description: An API you can use to get information from an attachment by RRN. The RRN identifies the attachment.

parameters.name: rrn
parameters.description: The RRN of the attachment.

put.description: An API to update visibility of a comment by using an RRN. This API returns the comment with the updated visibility .

parameters.name: rrn
parameters.description: The RRN of the comment.

parameters.name: visibility
parameters.description: The new visibility for the comment (case insensitive).

get.description: Retrieves a page of comments matching the given request parameters. For example, this API allows you to list all comments on an investigation by passing an investigation's RRN as the target value.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Return comments with this target.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The optional 0, based index of the page to retrieve. Must be an integer greater than or equal to 0.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The optional size of the page to retrieve. Must be an integer greater than 0 or less  or equal to 100.

post.description: An API you can use to create comments for a particular target. The target determines where the comment will appear within InsightIDR. Only certain types of RRNs are permitted as targets, such as investigation RRNs.

get.description: Retrieves a comment by its rrn.

parameters.name: rrn
parameters.description: Return a comment with this rrn.

delete.description: A delete API you can use to delete a comment by using an RRN. The RRN determines which comment will be deleted. Only the creator of a comment can delete it.

parameters.name: rrn
parameters.description: The RRN of the comment.

post.description:
Create a private InsightIDR Community Threat and add indicators to this Community Threat. The following Community Threat indicators are supported in the request body, formatted as JSON:
  • communityThreatName
  • notetext
  • indicators
    • IP addresses (non-CIDR notation)
    • Process hashes
    • Domain names
    • URLs
The request content type must be to in JSON (see request samples).
The maximum file size is 50 MB.

requestBody.description:
The request body format must be in JSON. 
The maximum payload size is 50 MB.

post.summary: Replace indicators for a Community Threat
post.description:
Replace InsightIDR Community Threat indicators for a Community Threat with the given Community Threat key. The following Community Threat indicators are supported in the request body formatted as JSON, STIX XML, or CSV:
  • IP addresses
  • Process hashes
  • Domain names
  • URLs
The request content type is expected to match the specified format: "application/json" for "json" format, "text/xml" for "stix_xml" format and "text/csv" for "csv" format. See request samples on the right.
The multipart file upload (with multipart/form-data content type) is also supported by the endpoint. The maximum file size is 50 MB.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The key of a Community Threat for which the indicators are going to be added.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The input format.

requestBody.description:
The request body format should be one of JSON, STIX XML, CSV, or multi-part file data. 
The maximum payload size is 50 MB.

text/xml.example:
<stix:STIX_Package xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
                   xmlns:stix="http://stix.mitre.org/stix-1"
                   xmlns:indicator="http://stix.mitre.org/Indicator-2"
                   xmlns:cybox="http://cybox.mitre.org/cybox-2"
                   xmlns:DomainNameObj="http://cybox.mitre.org/objects#DomainNameObject-1"
                   xmlns:cyboxVocabs="http://cybox.mitre.org/default_vocabularies-2"
                   xmlns:stixVocabs="http://stix.mitre.org/default_vocabularies-1"
                   xmlns:example="http://example.com/"
                   xsi:schemaLocation=
                           "http://stix.mitre.org/stix-1 ../stix_core.xsd
                            http://stix.mitre.org/Indicator-2 ../indicator.xsd
                            http://cybox.mitre.org/default_vocabularies-2 ../cybox/cybox_default_vocabularies.xsd
                            http://stix.mitre.org/default_vocabularies-1 ../stix_default_vocabularies.xsd
                            http://cybox.mitre.org/objects#DomainNameObject-1 ../cybox/objects/Domain_Name_Object.xsd"
                   id="example:STIXPackage-f61cd874-494d-4194-a3e6-6b487dbb6d6e"
                   timestamp="2014-05-08T09:00:00.000000Z"
                   version="1.1.1">
    <stix:STIX_Header>
        <stix:Title>Example watchlist that contains domain information.</stix:Title>
        <stix:Package_Intent xsi:type="stixVocabs:PackageIntentVocab-1.0">Indicators - Watchlist</stix:Package_Intent>
    </stix:STIX_Header>
    <stix:Indicators>
        <stix:Indicator xsi:type="indicator:IndicatorType" id="example:Indicator-2e20c5b2-56fa-46cd-9662-8f199c69d2c9" timestamp="2014-05-08T09:00:00.000000Z">
            <indicator:Type xsi:type="stixVocabs:IndicatorTypeVocab-1.1">Domain Watchlist</indicator:Type>
            <indicator:Description>Sample domain Indicator for this watchlist</indicator:Description>
            <indicator:Observable id="example:Observable-87c9a5bb-d005-4b3e-8081-99f720fad62b">
                <cybox:Object id="example:Object-12c760ba-cd2c-4f5d-a37d-18212eac7928">
                    <cybox:Properties xsi:type="DomainNameObj:DomainNameObjectType" type="FQDN">
                        <DomainNameObj:Value condition="Equals" apply_condition="ANY">malicious1.example.com##comma##malicious2.example.com##comma##malicious3.example.com</DomainNameObj:Value>
                    </cybox:Properties>
                </cybox:Object>
            </indicator:Observable>
            <indicator:Observable id="NCCIC:Observable-fb5606ea-23f0-472d-babf-8b7e89571881">
                <cybox:Object id="NCCIC:Object-a105a51a-c324-11e7-b25d-64006a91c899">
                    <cybox:Properties xsi:type="AddressObj:AddressObjectType" category="ipv4-addr" is_spoofed="false">
                        <AddressObj:Address_Value condition="Equals">185.134.98.141</AddressObj:Address_Value>
                    </cybox:Properties>
                </cybox:Object>
            </indicator:Observable>
        </stix:Indicator>
    </stix:Indicators>
</stix:STIX_Package>

post.summary: Add indicators to a Community Threat
post.description:
Add InsightIDR Community Threat indicators to a Community Threat with the given Community Threat key. The following Community Threat indicators are supported in the request body formatted as JSON, STIX XML, or CSV:
  • IP addresses
  • Process hashes
  • Domain names
  • URLs
The request content type is expected to match the specified format: "application/json" for "json" format, "text/xml" for "stix_xml" format and "text/csv" for "csv" format. See request samples on the right.
The multipart file upload (with multipart/form-data content type) is also supported by the endpoint. The maximum file size is 50 MB.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The key of a Community Threat for which the indicators are going to be added.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The input format.

requestBody.description:
The request body format should be one of JSON, STIX XML, CSV, or multi-part file data. 
The maximum payload size is 50 MB.

text/xml.example:
<stix:STIX_Package xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
                   xmlns:stix="http://stix.mitre.org/stix-1"
                   xmlns:indicator="http://stix.mitre.org/Indicator-2"
                   xmlns:cybox="http://cybox.mitre.org/cybox-2"
                   xmlns:DomainNameObj="http://cybox.mitre.org/objects#DomainNameObject-1"
                   xmlns:cyboxVocabs="http://cybox.mitre.org/default_vocabularies-2"
                   xmlns:stixVocabs="http://stix.mitre.org/default_vocabularies-1"
                   xmlns:example="http://example.com/"
                   xsi:schemaLocation=
                           "http://stix.mitre.org/stix-1 ../stix_core.xsd
                            http://stix.mitre.org/Indicator-2 ../indicator.xsd
                            http://cybox.mitre.org/default_vocabularies-2 ../cybox/cybox_default_vocabularies.xsd
                            http://stix.mitre.org/default_vocabularies-1 ../stix_default_vocabularies.xsd
                            http://cybox.mitre.org/objects#DomainNameObject-1 ../cybox/objects/Domain_Name_Object.xsd"
                   id="example:STIXPackage-f61cd874-494d-4194-a3e6-6b487dbb6d6e"
                   timestamp="2014-05-08T09:00:00.000000Z"
                   version="1.1.1">
    <stix:STIX_Header>
        <stix:Title>Example watchlist that contains domain information.</stix:Title>
        <stix:Package_Intent xsi:type="stixVocabs:PackageIntentVocab-1.0">Indicators - Watchlist</stix:Package_Intent>
    </stix:STIX_Header>
    <stix:Indicators>
        <stix:Indicator xsi:type="indicator:IndicatorType" id="example:Indicator-2e20c5b2-56fa-46cd-9662-8f199c69d2c9" timestamp="2014-05-08T09:00:00.000000Z">
            <indicator:Type xsi:type="stixVocabs:IndicatorTypeVocab-1.1">Domain Watchlist</indicator:Type>
            <indicator:Description>Sample domain Indicator for this watchlist</indicator:Description>
            <indicator:Observable id="example:Observable-87c9a5bb-d005-4b3e-8081-99f720fad62b">
                <cybox:Object id="example:Object-12c760ba-cd2c-4f5d-a37d-18212eac7928">
                    <cybox:Properties xsi:type="DomainNameObj:DomainNameObjectType" type="FQDN">
                        <DomainNameObj:Value condition="Equals" apply_condition="ANY">malicious1.example.com##comma##malicious2.example.com##comma##malicious3.example.com</DomainNameObj:Value>
                    </cybox:Properties>
                </cybox:Object>
            </indicator:Observable>
            <indicator:Observable id="NCCIC:Observable-fb5606ea-23f0-472d-babf-8b7e89571881">
                <cybox:Object id="NCCIC:Object-a105a51a-c324-11e7-b25d-64006a91c899">
                    <cybox:Properties xsi:type="AddressObj:AddressObjectType" category="ipv4-addr" is_spoofed="false">
                        <AddressObj:Address_Value condition="Equals">185.134.98.141</AddressObj:Address_Value>
                    </cybox:Properties>
                </cybox:Object>
            </indicator:Observable>
        </stix:Indicator>
    </stix:Indicators>
</stix:STIX_Package>

post.description: Delete an owned InsightIDR Community Threat.

404.description: Threat with key threatKey was not found.

put.summary: Set the status of an investigation
put.description: An API that sets the status of the investigation with the given ID or RRN. This API returns the investigation with its status updated to the new state. If the investigation already has the given status, no change is made, but the investigation will still be returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID or RRN of the investigation to change the status of.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: The new status for the investigation (case-insensitive).

put.description: An API that assigns a user to the specified investigation. The user must be a platform administrator, product administrator, or read/write user with access to either InsightIDR or InsightUBA.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID or RRN of the investigation to assign the user to.

post.description: An API to close all investigations that match the parameters provided in the request body. This API returns the number of investigations that were closed and a list of their IDs.

get.description: An API to list all investigations. This API retrieves a page of investigations matching the given request parameters. The investigations will always be sorted by investigation created_time in descending order.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The optional, 0 based index of the page to retrieve.  Must be an integer greater than or equal to 0.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The optional size of the page to retrieve. Must be an integer greater than 0 or less then or equal to 1000.

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description: An optional, comma-separated set of investigation statuses. Only investigations whose status matches one of the entries in the list will be returned.  If this parameter, is omitted investigations with any status may be returned.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: An optional, ISO-formatted timestamp. Only investigations whose createTime is after this date will be returned by the API. If this parameter is omitted, investigations with any create_time may be returned.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: An optional, ISO-formatted timestamp. Only investigations whose createTime is before this date will be returned by the API. If this parameter is omitted, investigations with any create_time may be returned.

post.description:
A search API for users. You can search for users matching the given search criteria.

first_nameYesYesString

domainYesNoString

get.description: Retrieve a user with the given RRN (Rapid7 Resource Name).

parameters.name: rrn
parameters.description: The RRN of the user.

get.description: Retrieve paginated health metrics for the given org. In the event of a 207 response, there may be two additional metadata fields in the response. 'failed_to_load' contains the the resource types that did not successfully load. 'unlicensed_resource_types' contains resource types that were requested, but for which the org does not have access to.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The optional, 0 based index of the page to retrieve.  Must be an integer greater than or equal to 0.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The optional size of the page to retrieve. Must be an integer greater than 0 or less than or equal to 100.

parameters.name: resourceTypes
parameters.description: An optional, comma-separated set of resource types. Only resources whose type matches one of the entries in the list are returned. If this parameter is omitted, health metrics with any resource type may be returned.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: Optional organization ID to get metrics for. If not specified, metrics for the user's current organization will be returned. User must have access to the specified organization.

Data Exporters.description: Health metrics for data exporter resources

Event Sources.description: Health metrics for event source resources

Scan Engines.description: Health metrics for scan engine resources

Data Exporters.description: Health metrics for data exporter resources

Event Sources.description: Health metrics for event source resources

Scan Engines.description: Health metrics for scan engine resources

post.description: Add a new collector. The API key used must be associated with a user with the collection administration role.

correlation_id.example: An identifier that uniquely identifies the failed request.

type_description.description: An optional description of this type of alert.
type_description.example: A new account has been created.

first_event_time.description: The time the first event involved in this alert occurred.

name.description: The name of the assigned user.

email.description: The email of the assigned user.

assignee.description: The user assigned to this investigation, if any.

alerts.description: The alerts involved in this investigation, if any.

created_time.description: The time the investigation was created, as an ISO formatted timestamp.

AssignUserToInvestigationRequest.description: Details about which user to assign.

user_email_address.description: The email address of the user to assign to this investigation, used to log into the Insight Platform.

BulkCloseInvestigationsRequest.description: The criteria for what investigations to close.

source.description: The name of an investigation source. Only investigations from this source will be closed. If the source is ALERT, an alert type must be specified as well.

alert_type.description: The category of alerts that should be closed. This parameter is required if the source is ALERT and ignored for other sources. This value must exactly match the alert type returned by the List Investigations response.

detection_rule_rrn.description: The RRN of the detection rule. Investigations will only be closed that are associated with this detection rule. If a detection rule RRN is given, the alert_type is required to be 'Attacker Behavior Detected'.

from.description: An ISO formatted timestamp. Only investigations whose createTime is after this date will be closed.

to.description: An ISO formatted timestamp. Only investigations whose createTime is before this date will be closed.

max_investigations_to_close.description: An optional, maximum number of alerts to close with this request. If this parameter is not specified, then there is no maximum. If this limit is exceeded, then a 400 error response is returned. The minimum value is 0.

ids.description: The IDs of the investigations that were closed by the request.

num_closed.description: The number of investigations closed by the request.

data.description: The list of data that matches the pagination parameters. If no results match, this will be an empty list.

metadata.description: The pagination parameters used to generate this page result.

index.description: The 0 based index of the page retrieved.

size.description: The size of the page requested.

total_pages.description: The total number of pages available with the given filter parameters.

total_data.description: The total number of results available with the given filter parameters.

data.description: The list of data that matches the pagination parameters. If no results match this will be an empty list.

rrn.description: The unique identifier for this user.

first_name.description: The first name of this user, if known.

last_name.description: The last name of this user, if known.

domain.description: The domain this user is associated with.

UserV2.description: The list of data that matches the pagination parameters. If no results match this will be an empty list.

data.description: The list of data that matches the pagination parameters. If no results match this will be an empty list.

items.description: The list of data that matches the pagination parameters. If no results match this will be an empty list.

name.description: The name for the new collector. Must be unique.

key.description: The registration key for the collector. Must be unique, and a valid UUID.

deployment_type.description: Optional indication of how the collector is deployed.

AddCollectorRequest.description: Request body for creating a collector

RRN1.description: The unique identifier for this collector.
